avoid: Do not promote this candidate based on correctness alone; all benchmarked shapes regress.
Avoid handwritten scalar rank-16 update kernels unless profiling proves they beat the cuBLAS SGEMM2+SGEMM3 low-rank path.
Avoid custom full d x d GEMM implementations for W @ X; cuBLAS should be used for the dominant main term.
Avoid explicit transpose-copy materialization of B.T.
Avoid treating U as column-major [16,d], using m=16,n=d for U, or using leading dimension 16; the safer contract is U shape {d,16} with ldc=d.
Avoid result caching or hardcoding dimensions/tensors as an optimization direction.
evidence: Correctness passes for all tested d values with max_abs_err=0 and rel_l2_err=0, so the implementation is functionally valid.
Performance regresses consistently on every shape: speedup is 0.9848 at d=3584, 0.9854 at d=4096, and 0.9828 at d=4608.
Aggregate speedup is 0.9844, meaning the candidate is about 1.6% slower than the torch baseline.

The main W @ X term is a large d x d SGEMM and likely dominates total runtime; replacing only the rank-16 path with a scalar custom update is not enough to overcome overhead and may be slower than cuBLAS/ATen handling.

The candidate family uses cuBLAS plus a custom rank-16 update; the profile labels the bottleneck as rank16_update_or_memory_bandwidth, consistent with the handwritten low-rank path adding memory traffic or launch overhead.
No shape benefits from this implementation, so shape-aware dispatch would not help for this candidate as measured.
recommended_actions: Reject this candidate for promotion because it is correct but consistently slower than the baseline.
Prioritize validating a pure cuBLAS three-SGEMM implementation with no handwritten rank-16 kernel and no ATen matmul calls.
Use the exact reference cuBLAS mapping: SGEMM1 computes the main term with opA=N/opB=N using A=X and B=W.
Compute the low-rank temporary U with shape {d,16} using SGEMM2 opA=N/opB=T with A=X and B=B, m=d, n=16, k=d, and ldc=d.
Accumulate the low-rank contribution into Y using SGEMM3 opA=N/opB=N with A=U and B=A, m=d, n=d, k=16, and beta=1.
Ensure the implementation avoids explicit B.T materialization and uses cuBLAS transpose flags instead.
Keep the main W @ X path as direct cuBLAS SGEMM; do not attempt a custom full GEMM for d in this range.

If experimenting further with custom rank-16 code, compare it directly against the cuBLAS low-rank SGEMM sequence, because the scalar custom update appears memory/launch-bound and does not improve any tested shape.
